title: Number of Ways to Assign Edge Weights II
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: Pareto-optimal challenge: the single Tarjan offline LCA solution is also the speed extreme and the memory extreme. Per the repo standard, no coincident solution-runtime.cpp or solution-memory.cpp is shipped.

variants.languageReason: The task is dominated by high-throughput tree/query adjacency scans, DSU path compression, iterative DFS, and modular integer writes at n,q <= 1e5. C++ gives compact contiguous storage, direct vector<vector<int>> LeetCode integration, and native branch-light loops without managed-runtime or interpreter overhead.

languageAlternatives.assessment: Selected. It best combines Tarjan offline LCA performance, compact forward-star arrays, fast DSU operations, direct method signature support, and predictable memory usage.

Considered for manual arrays and a low runtime baseline, but LeetCode's C nested-array and return-buffer plumbing adds complexity without reducing the O(n + q) auxiliary footprint below the C++ version.
Considered for native speed and memory safety, but the mutable iterative DFS, DSU, and adjacency indexing add wrapper and bounds-check costs without improving the algorithmic profile.
Considered for compiled loops and slices, but bounds checks, runtime metadata, and GC interaction are less favorable for top-percentile adjacency and DSU workloads.
Considered because primitive arrays can be fast after JIT warmup, but managed runtime startup, object baselines, and GC overhead are unnecessary for this linear native workload.
Considered for concise implementation, but interpreter/VM overhead is too high for 1e5-node DFS plus 1e5 LCA queries under a top 1% target.

variants.approachJustification: Use the closed-form parity count 2^(distance-1) for non-empty paths, and compute all path distances with iterative Tarjan offline LCA in near-linear time and linear memory.

reasoningSummary: A path cost is odd exactly when an odd number of path edges receive weight 1, so a path with d > 0 edges has 2^(d-1) valid assignments and d = 0 has none. The whole problem becomes batched tree distance queries; Tarjan offline LCA gives every LCA in near-linear time and linear memory while an iterative DFS avoids stack overflow.
